one_time_description: Subsequent to year end, Band received $11,866,645 from Ktunaxa Nation Council Society as part of Internal Revenue Sharing Agreement - Mineral Tax payment. Also, large 'Other income' of $14,611,508 in current year vs $2,940,877 prior year suggests significant one-time income in the year.
economic_notes: Band operates multiple business enterprises including Ainsworth Hot Springs Ltd., Ainsworth Hot Springs Limited Partnership, Ka'amaknala Development Corporation, Lower Kootenay Development Limited Partnership, Lower Kootenay Development Corporation, Morris Flowers Inc., Nupika wu'u GP Ltd., and yaqan nukiy Farms LP. Subsequent to year end received $11,866,645 mineral tax revenue sharing from Ktunaxa Nation Council Society. Also has investments in All Nations Trust Company, Ktunaxa Holdings LP, Nupqu Development Corporation, SEM Holdings, SEM Resort, BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-adoption of PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations - management did not complete assessment. Lower Kootenay Guide Outfitters Ltd. is fully consolidated. Six GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. Large spike in 'Other income' ($14.6M vs $2.9M prior year) likely related to Impact Management and Benefits Agreement funding or similar. Subsequent event: $11.87M mineral tax revenue sharing received after year end; $5.055M loan repaid to BMO after year end. Expenses by segment show Administration, Social Development, Health, Education, Social Housing, and Capital but not separate infrastructure line - infrastructure/land management expenses of $1,933,779 appear to be within Administration segment in segmented note. Population noted as 'over 200 members'. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text.
